_meta.description: Question database for Solace Browser (local AI agent browser) — 47-persona committee
_meta.doctrine: Each bug is a missing question. Max questions = Max love = Max QA quality.

questions.question: What happens when Playwright loses connection to the browser process mid-navigation?
questions.motivation: The browser automation layer can fail silently. What does solace-browser do when Playwright disconnects?
questions.oracle: SFDIPOT-Platform: kill the browser process mid-navigation — does the error propagate to the user or hang?

questions.question: Can a malicious web page accessed via Solace Browser exfiltrate data from other tabs or local files?
questions.motivation: Playwright's browser context isolation — does a malicious page in Tab A affect Tab B or local ~/.solace/?
questions.oracle: Security oracle: open malicious test page in Solace Browser — can it read localStorage of other tabs?

questions.question: What test would tell us, immediately, if the YinYang delight engine is broken?
questions.motivation: YinYang is the personality layer. If it stops working, users lose delight but the app 'works'. Silent degradation.
questions.oracle: TDD oracle: POST /api/yinyang/status → verify {active: true, joke_count: 100, personality: set}

questions.question: Can an attacker control what Solace Browser navigates to via the YinYang notification API?
questions.motivation: POST /api/yinyang/notify lets agents push navigation commands. Can an external attacker inject malicious URLs?
questions.oracle: OWASP oracle: POST /api/yinyang/notify with message containing 'navigate:file:///etc/passwd' — what happens?

questions.question: What body language does the YinYang rotating GIF communicate? Trustworthy? Playful? Threatening?
questions.motivation: Users anthropomorphize AI. The YinYang visual sets the emotional contract. Is it the right one?
questions.oracle: EQ oracle: show the YinYang GIF to 10 people. Ask: 'What personality does this represent?'

questions.question: What micro-expressions would users show when they first see the YinYang tutorial?
questions.motivation: Delight vs confusion vs skepticism in the first 2 seconds of the tutorial determines completion rate
questions.oracle: EQ oracle: screen-record user faces during first tutorial step. Are they smiling? Frowning? Confused?

questions.question: Is the friction in the sign-up flow a bug or a feature? Does it filter low-commitment users?
questions.motivation: High friction filters out users who won't pay. Some friction = self-selection of committed users
questions.oracle: Behavioral oracle: A/B test — reduce friction by 50% — does conversion improve but churn increase?

questions.question: What happens when two browser windows run different recipes simultaneously?
questions.motivation: Concurrency in browser automation: two recipes modifying the same page state = race condition
questions.oracle: SFDIPOT-Time: launch Recipe A (Gmail) and Recipe B (Slack) simultaneously — do they interfere?

questions.question: Who is Solace Browser for? AI developers? Non-technical users? Power users? 'Everyone' is not an answer.
questions.motivation: Marketing clarity: the app store, tutorial, and YinYang tone need to match a specific user
questions.oracle: Claims oracle: read solaceagi.com/agents — who is the intended user? Is it specific enough to resonate?

questions.question: Do the 18 app tiles in the app store actually work? Or do they install and silently fail?
questions.motivation: App store claims 18 apps. How many of them actually work end-to-end? Have we tested them all?
questions.oracle: RST oracle: install each of the 18 apps, run their primary workflow, observe output

questions.question: What is the CPU profile of YinYang animation? Is it wasting cycles on the main thread?
questions.motivation: 60fps animation requires <16ms per frame. YinYang rotating GIF + CSS animations may block the main thread
questions.oracle: Perf oracle: DevTools CPU profiler during YinYang tutorial — are there long tasks (>50ms)?

questions.question: Are there memory leaks in the Playwright browser workers?
questions.motivation: Long-running Playwright sessions accumulate memory. After 8 hours of use, does memory grow unboundedly?
questions.oracle: Memory oracle: run 1000 page navigations, measure memory before/after each 100

questions.question: Does the MCP server expose any data to external clients that shouldn't be accessible?
questions.motivation: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a new protocol. Its security model is not well-audited
questions.oracle: Security oracle: send arbitrary MCP tool calls to mcp_server.py — can you read local files?

questions.question: What configuration combinations of YinYang personality × locale have never been tested?
questions.motivation: 13 locales × 5 personality modes = 65 combinations. We've probably tested 5.
questions.oracle: Variability tour: test Arabic locale + Minimal personality. Japanese locale + Friendly personality.

questions.question: Is the browser state mutable in dangerous ways? What state mutations are we not tracking?
questions.motivation: Playwright browser state (cookies, localStorage, session) changes between recipes. Are we managing this correctly?
questions.oracle: State oracle: run Recipe A, then run Recipe B — does B see A's leftover state?

questions.question: What happens if the OAuth3 vault file (~/.solace/vault.enc) is corrupted or deleted?
questions.motivation: Single point of failure: if the vault is gone, the user loses all tokens. Is there a recovery flow?
questions.oracle: Resilience oracle: delete vault.enc — what happens? Can the user recover without losing all tokens?

questions.question: What's the most jaw-dropping use case we could demonstrate in 60 seconds?
questions.motivation: A viral demo needs one moment that makes viewers say 'That's impossible. How did it do that?'
questions.oracle: MrBeast oracle: record a 60-second demo of Solace Browser. Show to 10 non-technical users.

questions.question: What personality type is most likely to be early adopter of Solace Browser? How do we design for them?
questions.motivation: Early adopters have specific personality profiles. Designing for them accelerates word-of-mouth
questions.oracle: User oracle: survey first 100 sign-ups for Big 5 personality. Is there a pattern?

questions.question: Are the 7 MCP tools (navigate, click, fill, screenshot, snapshot, evaluate, aria_snapshot) all working correctly?
questions.motivation: MCP server exposes 7 tools. Each has its own failure mode. Are they all tested end-to-end?
questions.oracle: MCP oracle: call each of the 7 tools via JSON-RPC — verify response structure and side effects
questions.answer: test-spec-mcp-server.json covers tools/list endpoint — returns all 7 tools (GLOW 97)

questions.question: Do all 13 locale translations accurately represent the meaning in the source English?
questions.motivation: A bad translation creates a different product for 12 of 13 locales. Arabic RTL layout + wrong words = broken
questions.oracle: i18n oracle: back-translate 5 random strings from each locale back to English — does meaning match?

questions.question: What happens to Solace Browser when the YinYang chat backend (OpenRouter) is down?
questions.motivation: Third-party dependency failure: if OpenRouter is down, does YinYang silently fail or gracefully degrade?
questions.oracle: Resilience oracle: block traffic to api.openrouter.ai — what does YinYang show the user?

questions.question: Is 'automatic' browser control perceived as trustworthy or terrifying by users?
questions.motivation: Watching a browser navigate 'by itself' triggers fear in most users. How do we make this feel safe?
questions.oracle: Behavioral oracle: record user reactions when watching Solace Browser navigate Gmail automatically

questions.question: Does the Solace Browser web server (web/server.py) handle concurrent requests safely?
questions.motivation: Flask/FastAPI: if two recipe runs arrive simultaneously on port 8791, do they interfere?
questions.oracle: Concurrency oracle: fire 20 simultaneous POST /api/navigate requests — all should return distinct results

questions.question: Is the app store accessible to users with visual disabilities? WCAG 2.2 AA compliance?
questions.motivation: The 18-tile grid uses emoji, color, small text. Blind users + screen reader = broken experience
questions.oracle: WCAG oracle: run axe-core on app-store.html — how many violations?

questions.question: Is the Solace Inspector itself tested? Who watches the watchman?
questions.motivation: If the Inspector has bugs, all QA reports are wrong. Meta-testing the test tool is critical
questions.oracle: Meta-oracle: run the Inspector against itself (its own web UI at 127.0.0.1:8791). Does it score correctly?
questions.answer: GLOW 93: self-diagnostic 7/7 specs 100% Green. Inspector tests itself. Evidence sealed.

questions.question: [FROM BUG F-002] What happens if a required JSON key is absent in recipe/content data?
questions.motivation: Bug F-002: blog post missing 'image' key caused BROKEN-1 false positive. Schema validation missing.
questions.oracle: Validation oracle: remove required keys from blog JSON — does the system fail gracefully or silently break?
questions.answer: Fixed in GLOW 96. Image key added to prime-wiki post. Next: add schema validation for all content JSON.

questions.question: [FROM BUG F-003] Are all binary assets that appear in production tracked in version control?
questions.motivation: Bug F-003: gallery images existed locally but not in git → 404 on production. Any untracked asset = broken prod.
questions.oracle: Asset oracle: for every in HTML, verify the asset exists in git (not just on disk)
questions.answer: Fixed in GLOW 96. Gallery images added to git. web/images/gallery/ now tracked.

questions.question: [FROM BUG BROKEN-1] Does the broken image detector account for intentionally hidden images?
questions.motivation: Bug: lightbox placeholders with display:none parent triggered BROKEN-1 false positives.
questions.oracle: SFDIPOT-Function: verify BROKEN-1 heuristic skips images whose parent has display:none
questions.answer: Fixed in GLOW 93. Heuristic now checks parent visibility before flagging broken images.

questions.question: What is the One Thing the tutorial is trying to get the user to do? Is it crystal clear?
questions.motivation: The 5-step YinYang tutorial must have a single, clear call-to-action at the end. What is it?
questions.oracle: Funnel oracle: read the last step of the tutorial — what does it ask the user to do next?

questions.question: Is there a moment in the tutorial where users say 'Wait, how did it do THAT?'
questions.motivation: Tutorial must have at least one wow moment or users won't complete it (or tell anyone)
questions.oracle: MrBeast oracle: watch users complete the tutorial — at what point do they sit up and lean forward?

questions.question: Are the fun pack JSON files validated before loading? What happens with malformed fun packs?
questions.motivation: Community fun packs could be malformed or malicious. Loading without validation = crash or injection
questions.oracle: Data oracle: load a fun pack with malformed JSON — does the system crash or fail gracefully?

questions.question: Does the GDPR 'right to be forgotten' apply to Solace Browser's local data? How does a user delete everything?
questions.motivation: ~/.solace/ contains PII (email, tokens, evidence). How does a user completely erase their local data?
questions.oracle: Statutes oracle: is there a 'Delete All My Data' button or command? Does it actually delete everything?

questions.question: What's the risk reversal for Solace Browser? Why would anyone hesitate to try it?
questions.motivation: Grand Slam Offer: eliminate all reasons not to try. What are the real objections? Security? Complexity? Cost?
questions.oracle: Business oracle: ask 5 non-adopters 'Why haven't you tried it?' — categorize and count responses

questions.question: Are there any hardcoded API keys, tokens, or credentials remaining in the public repo?
questions.motivation: Public repo means automated scrapers will find secrets within minutes of visibility change.
questions.oracle: git log --all -p | grep -iE '(api_key|secret|token|password|credential)' — zero matches expected
questions.answer: AUDITED in GLOW 122: grep found zero API keys. Private emails redacted. COGS data moved to scratch/. Pricing diagram moved to scratch/.

questions.question: Does the git history contain any secrets that were committed and later removed?
questions.motivation: git log preserves everything. A secret committed in commit A and deleted in commit B is still in history.
questions.oracle: git log --all --diff-filter=D -p | grep -iE '(api_key|secret|password)' — check deleted file diffs
questions.answer: AUDITED in GLOW 122: No API keys found in git history. Private emails existed in history but repo was recreated clean before public release.

questions.question: Does the FSL license clearly prevent competitors from forking and stripping safety rails?
questions.motivation: The whole point of Source-Available is preventing OpenClaw-style malicious forks. Is the LICENSE file enforceable?
questions.oracle: Read LICENSE file — Competing Use clause must explicitly prohibit products that compete with Solace Browser

questions.question: Does CONTRIBUTING.md make first-time contributors feel welcome and know exactly where to start?
questions.motivation: Source-Available still accepts contributions. The onboarding experience determines contribution rate.
questions.oracle: Read CONTRIBUTING.md — does it have clear steps, development setup, and friendly tone?

questions.question: Does SECURITY.md provide a clear vulnerability reporting path that discourages public disclosure?
questions.motivation: Public repos get security researchers. If SECURITY.md is unclear, they'll open public issues instead of private reports.
questions.oracle: Read SECURITY.md — must have email contact, NOT GitHub issues, with response time commitment

questions.question: Is the Inspector app directory fully removed from solace-browser or does residual state leak?
questions.motivation: Inspector was moved to solace-cli. Any leftover files in solace-browser are trade secret leakage.
questions.oracle: ls data/default/apps/solace-inspector/ in solace-browser — should not exist or be in scratch/

questions.question: Does the README clearly communicate what makes Solace Browser different from Browser-Use, Bardeen, and OpenClaw?
questions.motivation: Public repo = first impression for developers. If README doesn't differentiate, visitors leave in 10 seconds.
questions.oracle: Read README.md — must mention OAuth3, evidence trails, recipe system, and FSL within first 3 sections
